en_definition: to take and use something that belongs to someone else and return it later
example1: Can I borrow your pen for a minute, please?
example1_ru: Можно я одолжу твою ручку на минутку, пожалуйста?
example2: She borrowed three books from the library last week.

en_definition: to give something to someone for a period of time, expecting it back
example1: Could you lend me some money until Friday?
example1_ru: Не мог бы ты одолжить мне немного денег до пятницы?
example2: He lent his umbrella to a classmate in the rain.

en_definition: a woman who owns a house or flat and rents it to tenants
example1: Our landlady fixed the broken heater last night.
example1_ru: Наша хозяйка квартиры починила сломанный обогреватель прошлой ночью.
example2: The landlady asked us to pay rent by the fifth.

en_definition: a person you share a room or flat with
example1: My roommate always leaves the light on at night.
example1_ru: Мой сосед по комнате всегда оставляет свет включённым на ночь.
example2: We found a new roommate through the university website.

en_definition: a document showing how much you owe for water, gas, or electricity
example1: The utility bill arrived a week earlier than usual.
example1_ru: Счёт за коммунальные услуги пришёл на неделю раньше обычного.
example2: We split the utility bill equally between three roommates.

en_definition: a written contract between a tenant and the owner of a property
example1: We signed the lease agreement for one full year.
example1_ru: Мы подписали договор аренды на целый год.
example2: Read the lease agreement carefully before you sign it.

en_definition: to start living in a new home
example1: We plan to move in next Monday morning.
example1_ru: Мы планируем въехать в следующий понедельник утром.
example2: They moved in together after finding a cheap flat.

en_definition: to leave a home you were living in
example1: We have to move out before the end of June.
example1_ru: Нам нужно съехать до конца июня.
example2: He moved out of the dorm after his first year.

en_definition: a person whose job is to fix water pipes and taps
example1: We called a plumber because the sink was leaking.
example1_ru: Мы вызвали сантехника, потому что раковина протекала.
example2: The plumber fixed the shower in under an hour.

en_definition: a small hole or crack that lets liquid or gas escape
example1: There is a water leak under the kitchen sink.
example1_ru: Под кухонной раковиной есть утечка воды.
example2: The gas leak was fixed before anyone got hurt.

en_definition: a person who looks after a building and helps with repairs
example1: The caretaker fixed the broken lock on our door.
example1_ru: Смотритель починил сломанный замок на нашей двери.
example2: Ask the caretaker if the water is turned off today.

en_definition: a small form you fill in when you pay money into a bank account
example1: Keep the deposit slip until the payment is confirmed.
example1_ru: Сохраните квитанцию о депозите, пока платёж не подтвердится.
example2: She lost her deposit slip and had to ask for a copy.

en_definition: money in the form of coins and notes, not cards
example1: I only have a little cash on me today.
example1_ru: У меня сегодня с собой совсем немного наличных.
example2: Many small shops here only accept cash.

en_definition: the coins you get back after paying more than the price
example1: Don't forget to take your change from the cashier.
example1_ru: Не забудь взять сдачу у кассира.
example2: He counted his change before leaving the shop.

en_definition: a small flat case for carrying money and cards
example1: I left my wallet at home this morning.
example1_ru: Я оставил кошелёк дома этим утром.
example2: She keeps her student card in her wallet.

en_definition: a small bag used mainly by women to carry money and small items
example1: She checked her purse for the bus fare.
example1_ru: Она проверила сумочку на наличие денег на автобус.
example2: His grandmother gave him a coin purse as a gift.

en_definition: a place or service where you change one country's money for another's
example1: We found a currency exchange near the airport.
example1_ru: Мы нашли обмен валюты рядом с аэропортом.
example2: The currency exchange rate changes every single day.

en_definition: an amount of money you must pay for a service
example1: There is a small fee for using this machine.
example1_ru: За использование этой машины взимается небольшая плата.
example2: The university charges an application fee every year.

en_definition: to ask someone to pay more money than something is really worth
example1: The taxi driver tried to overcharge us for the ride.
example1_ru: Таксист попытался взять с нас слишком много за поездку.
example2: Some tourists get overcharged in busy markets.

en_definition: to have enough money to pay for something
example1: We can't afford a new laptop right now.
example1_ru: Мы не можем позволить себе новый ноутбук прямо сейчас.
example2: She saved for months to afford the trip.

en_definition: one of several regular payments made over time
example1: He is paying for his phone in monthly installments.
example1_ru: Он платит за телефон ежемесячными взносами.
example2: The first installment is due next week.

en_definition: a shop that sells food and household items
example1: There is a grocery store just around the corner.
example1_ru: Продуктовый магазин прямо за углом.
example2: We buy vegetables from the grocery store every weekend.

en_definition: the place in a shop where you pay for items
example1: The line at the checkout counter was very long.
example1_ru: Очередь на кассе была очень длинной.
example2: She waited five minutes at the checkout counter.

en_definition: a container used for carrying items while shopping
example1: He filled his basket with fruit and bread.
example1_ru: Он наполнил свою корзину фруктами и хлебом.
example2: Leave your basket by the door when you finish.

en_definition: a wheeled cart used to carry shopping in a store
example1: She pushed the trolley through the crowded aisles.
example1_ru: Она толкала тележку по переполненным проходам.
example2: Put the heavy bottles at the bottom of the trolley.

en_definition: a passage between rows of shelves in a shop
example1: The bread is in the third aisle on the left.
example1_ru: Хлеб в третьем проходе слева.
example2: He got lost between the aisles of the supermarket.

en_definition: a sale where a shop reduces prices to sell items quickly
example1: We bought winter coats at the clearance sale.
example1_ru: Мы купили зимние куртки на распродаже со скидками.
example2: The clearance sale ends this Sunday evening.

en_definition: able to be paid back if you are not satisfied
example1: The ticket is refundable if you cancel in time.
example1_ru: Билет подлежит возврату, если вы отмените его вовремя.
example2: Check whether the deposit is refundable before you pay.

en_definition: to give back a bought item and get a different one instead
example1: I want to exchange this shirt for a bigger size.
example1_ru: Я хочу обменять эту рубашку на больший размер.
example2: You can exchange the shoes within thirty days.

en_definition: a table that helps you choose the right clothing size
example1: Check the size chart before ordering online.
example1_ru: Проверьте таблицу размеров перед заказом онлайн.
example2: The size chart is printed on the back of the tag.

en_definition: a small piece of paper or plastic attached to an item showing its price
example1: The price tag was still on the new jacket.
example1_ru: Ценник всё ещё был на новой куртке.
example2: Cut off the tag before you wear the sweater.

en_definition: a shop that sells fresh bread and cakes
example1: The smell of fresh bread came from the bakery.
example1_ru: Запах свежего хлеба доносился из пекарни.
example2: We buy croissants from the bakery every morning.

en_definition: remaining after the rest has been used or eaten
example1: We had leftover pizza for lunch the next day.
example1_ru: На следующий день на обед у нас была оставшаяся пицца.
example2: Put the leftover rice in the fridge tonight.

en_definition: no longer fresh, especially bread that has become hard
example1: This bread tastes stale; buy a fresh loaf tomorrow.
example1_ru: Этот хлеб на вкус чёрствый; купи свежую буханку завтра.
example2: The chips went stale after being left open.

en_definition: food bought at a restaurant to eat somewhere else
example1: We ordered a takeaway because we were too tired to cook.
example1_ru: Мы заказали еду навынос, потому что слишком устали готовить.
example2: The takeaway arrived faster than we expected.

en_definition: a place in a school or workplace where meals are sold
example1: Students often eat lunch in the university canteen.
example1_ru: Студенты часто обедают в университетской столовой.
example2: The canteen serves rice and noodles every day.

en_definition: the amount of food served to one person
example1: The portion size here is bigger than at home.
example1_ru: Размер порции здесь больше, чем дома.
example2: She asked for a smaller portion size of rice.

en_definition: having a strong, hot taste from spices
example1: This soup is too spicy for me to finish.
example1_ru: Этот суп слишком острый, чтобы я мог его доесть.
example2: He loves spicy food more than sweet dishes.

en_definition: having a sharp taste like lemon
example1: The lemon juice made the drink taste quite sour.
example1_ru: Лимонный сок сделал напиток довольно кислым на вкус.
example2: This yogurt is a bit too sour for my taste.

en_definition: having a very pleasant taste
example1: This homemade soup is absolutely delicious.
example1_ru: Этот домашний суп абсолютно восхитителен.
example2: Everyone said the birthday cake was delicious.

en_definition: having very little or no flavor
example1: The soup was tasteless without any salt.
example1_ru: Суп был безвкусным без соли.
example2: He complained that the rice was tasteless.

en_definition: fully grown and ready to eat, used for fruit
example1: These bananas are not ripe enough yet.
example1_ru: Эти бананы ещё недостаточно спелые.
example2: Choose a ripe mango by smelling its skin.

en_definition: to remove the outer skin of a fruit or vegetable
example1: Can you peel the potatoes for dinner tonight?
example1_ru: Ты можешь почистить картошку к сегодняшнему ужину?
example2: She peeled an orange and shared it with us.

en_definition: to cut something into thin flat pieces
example1: He sliced the bread for breakfast sandwiches.
example1_ru: Он нарезал хлеб для бутербродов на завтрак.
example2: Slice the tomatoes thinly before adding them to the salad.

en_definition: a dish cooked quickly in a small amount of hot oil
example1: We made a vegetable stir-fry for dinner.
example1_ru: Мы приготовили овощное жаркое на ужин.
example2: The chef added soy sauce to the stir-fry.

en_definition: to cook food using hot water vapor
example1: It's healthier to steam vegetables than to fry them.
example1_ru: Готовить овощи на пару полезнее, чем жарить.
example2: She steamed the fish with ginger and spring onion.

en_definition: salt, pepper, or spices added to food for flavor
example1: Add a little seasoning before you serve the dish.
example1_ru: Добавьте немного приправы перед подачей блюда.
example2: This dish needs more seasoning to taste better.

en_definition: a machine used at home for a particular job, like cooking
example1: The kitchen has several modern electrical appliances.
example1_ru: На кухне есть несколько современных электроприборов.
example2: This appliance store sells kettles and toasters.

en_definition: a container used to boil water
example1: I boiled water in the kettle for tea.
example1_ru: Я вскипятил воду в чайнике для чая.
example2: The kettle switches off automatically when it boils.

en_definition: a tool with a long handle used to clean floors
example1: He used a mop to clean the kitchen floor.
example1_ru: Он использовал швабру, чтобы помыть пол на кухне.
example2: The mop was left drying in the bathroom.

en_definition: a tool used for sweeping dust and dirt from floors
example1: She swept the balcony with an old broom.
example1_ru: Она подмела балкон старой метлой.
example2: We keep the broom behind the kitchen door.

en_definition: a place with washing machines that people pay to use
example1: There is a laundromat next to our dormitory.
example1_ru: Рядом с нашим общежитием есть прачечная самообслуживания.
example2: We wash our clothes at the laundromat every weekend.

en_definition: a chemical substance used for washing clothes or dishes
example1: We ran out of laundry detergent this morning.
example1_ru: У нас закончилось стиральное моющее средство этим утром.
example2: Add a little detergent before starting the washing machine.

en_definition: a narrow table used for ironing clothes
example1: The ironing board is folded behind the door.
example1_ru: Гладильная доска сложена за дверью.
example2: Set up the ironing board before you start.

en_definition: fine dry powder that collects on surfaces
example1: There is a thick layer of dust on the shelf.
example1_ru: На полке толстый слой пыли.
example2: Please wipe the dust off the table before dinner.

en_definition: to make a device start working by pressing a button
example1: Please switch on the lights; it's getting dark.
example1_ru: Пожалуйста, включи свет, темнеет.
example2: He switched on the heater when it got cold.

en_definition: to make a device stop working by pressing a button
example1: Remember to switch off the fan before you leave.
example1_ru: Не забудь выключить вентилятор перед уходом.
example2: She switched off the TV and went to bed.

en_definition: to connect an electrical device to a power supply
example1: Plug in the charger before you go to sleep.
example1_ru: Включи зарядку в розетку перед сном.
example2: He plugged in his laptop near the desk.

en_definition: a socket in a wall where you connect electrical devices
example1: There is only one outlet in this room.
example1_ru: В этой комнате только одна розетка.
example2: I need an adapter for the foreign outlet.

en_definition: a person who regularly travels between home and work or school
example1: The train was full of tired commuters this morning.
example1_ru: Поезд был полон усталых пассажиров, едущих на работу, этим утром.
example2: Many commuters prefer the subway to save time.

en_definition: a card used to pay for bus, train, or subway trips
example1: Top up your fare card before entering the station.
example1_ru: Пополни проездной билет перед входом на станцию.
example2: I lost my fare card somewhere on the bus.

en_definition: the number shown for a specific railway track at a station
example1: Check the screen for your train's track number.
example1_ru: Проверь на экране номер пути своего поезда.
example2: Our track number changed just before departure.

en_definition: a display showing arrival and departure times
example1: Check the timetable board for the next bus.
example1_ru: Посмотри на табло расписания насчёт следующего автобуса.
example2: The timetable board showed a ten-minute delay.

en_definition: a second flight you take after landing to reach your final destination
example1: We missed our connecting flight because of the delay.
example1_ru: Мы пропустили стыковочный рейс из-за задержки.
example2: Make sure you have enough time for a connecting flight.

en_definition: a short stop between two parts of a journey, especially by plane
example1: We had a six-hour layover in Dubai.
example1_ru: У нас была шестичасовая пересадка в Дубае.
example2: During the layover, we grabbed some coffee at the airport.

en_definition: a document that allows you to get on a plane
example1: Please have your boarding pass and passport ready.
example1_ru: Пожалуйста, приготовьте посадочный талон и паспорт.
example2: She scanned her boarding pass at the gate.

en_definition: the place in an airport where passengers board a plane
example1: Our gate changed twenty minutes before boarding.
example1_ru: Наш выход на посадку изменился за двадцать минут до посадки.
example2: The announcement said to go to gate fifteen.

en_definition: a person who checks bags and documents at a border
example1: The customs officer asked to see my passport.
example1_ru: Таможенник попросил показать мой паспорт.
example2: We waited in line for the customs officer.

en_definition: the area in an airport where you collect your luggage
example1: We waited an hour at the baggage claim.
example1_ru: Мы ждали час у выдачи багажа.
example2: Head to the baggage claim after passing customs.

en_definition: a small bag you take onto a plane instead of checking it
example1: Pack your laptop in your carry-on, not your suitcase.
example1_ru: Положи ноутбук в ручную кладь, а не в чемодан.
example2: Airlines usually allow one carry-on and one small bag.

en_definition: tiredness caused by traveling quickly across time zones
example1: I had terrible jet lag after the long flight.
example1_ru: У меня была ужасная усталость от смены часовых поясов после долгого перелёта.
example2: It took a few days to recover from jet lag.

en_definition: a region where the same standard time is used
example1: China and Russia are in different time zones.
example1_ru: Китай и Россия находятся в разных часовых поясах.
example2: I always forget to adjust for the new time zone.

en_definition: the time in the place where you currently are
example1: We landed at ten o'clock local time.
example1_ru: Мы приземлились в десять часов по местному времени.
example2: Please set your watch to local time.

en_definition: a tool or app used to change one currency into another
example1: I used a currency converter to check the exchange rate.
example1_ru: Я использовал конвертер валют, чтобы проверить курс обмена.
example2: The currency converter app updates prices every hour.

en_definition: a small book with useful phrases in a foreign language
example1: She packed a phrasebook for her trip to China.
example1_ru: Она взяла разговорник для поездки в Китай.
example2: The phrasebook helped him order food in a new language.

en_definition: a place that many visitors go to see
example1: The old bridge is the city's main tourist attraction.
example1_ru: Старый мост — главная туристическая достопримечательность города.
example2: We visited two tourist attractions before lunch.

en_definition: the activity of visiting interesting places as a tourist
example1: We spent the whole weekend sightseeing around the old town.
example1_ru: Мы провели все выходные, осматривая достопримечательности старого города.
example2: Sightseeing is tiring but really enjoyable.

en_definition: a phone application that shows maps and directions
example1: I used a map app to find the nearest pharmacy.
example1_ru: Я использовал приложение-карту, чтобы найти ближайшую аптеку.
example2: The map app showed the fastest route to campus.

en_definition: to not know where you are or how to reach your destination
example1: We got lost while trying to find the train station.
example1_ru: Мы заблудились, пытаясь найти вокзал.
example2: It's easy to get lost in a new city at night.

en_definition: to ask someone how to get to a place
example1: He stopped to ask directions to the museum.
example1_ru: Он остановился, чтобы спросить дорогу до музея.
example2: Don't be afraid to ask directions if you're unsure.

en_definition: to change direction toward the left side
example1: Turn left at the next traffic light.
example1_ru: Поверните налево на следующем светофоре.
example2: You need to turn left after the bridge.

en_definition: to continue in the same direction without turning
example1: Go straight for two blocks and then turn right.
example1_ru: Идите прямо два квартала, потом поверните направо.
example2: Just go straight until you see the library.

en_definition: a marked place on the road where people can cross safely
example1: Always use the crosswalk when crossing a busy street.
example1_ru: Всегда используй пешеходный переход, переходя оживлённую улицу.
example2: The car stopped to let us use the crosswalk.

en_definition: the path beside a road for people walking
example1: Bicycles are not allowed on the sidewalk here.
example1_ru: Велосипеды здесь не разрешены на тротуаре.
example2: We walked along the sidewalk to the bus stop.

en_definition: a long line of vehicles that cannot move quickly
example1: We were stuck in a traffic jam for an hour.
example1_ru: Мы застряли в пробке на час.
example2: The traffic jam made us late for class.

en_definition: the time of day when traffic and public transport are busiest
example1: Avoid the subway during rush hour if you can.
example1_ru: Избегай метро в час пик, если можешь.
example2: Rush hour in this city starts around six in the evening.

en_definition: a person who is walking, not driving or riding
example1: Drivers must stop for pedestrians at the crossing.
example1_ru: Водители должны останавливаться перед пешеходами на переходе.
example2: The street was full of pedestrians during the festival.

en_definition: a place where people go to see a doctor for small health problems
example1: The campus clinic is open until six in the evening.
example1_ru: Университетская поликлиника открыта до шести вечера.
example2: I made an appointment at the clinic for Tuesday.

en_definition: an arranged time to meet someone, especially a doctor
example1: I have a dentist appointment tomorrow morning.
example1_ru: У меня завтра утром запись к стоматологу.
example2: She booked an appointment with the doctor online.

en_definition: when a doctor tells you what medicine to take
example1: The doctor prescribed some tablets for my cold.
example1_ru: Врач прописал мне таблетки от простуды.
example2: He was prescribed rest and plenty of water.

en_definition: a medicine that reduces pain
example1: I took a painkiller for my headache.
example1_ru: Я принял обезболивающее от головной боли.
example2: The nurse gave her a mild painkiller.

en_definition: a short period of feeling that everything is spinning
example1: She had a dizzy spell after standing up too fast.
example1_ru: У неё случился приступ головокружения после того, как она резко встала.
example2: A dizzy spell forced him to sit down immediately.

en_definition: a body temperature that is higher than normal
example1: He stayed home from class because of a fever.
example1_ru: Он остался дома вместо занятий из-за температуры.
example2: The medicine helped bring her fever down.

en_definition: a tool that helps you understand what illness your symptoms might mean
example1: I used a symptom checker before calling the clinic.
example1_ru: Я воспользовался проверкой симптомов перед звонком в клинику.
example2: The app's symptom checker suggested seeing a doctor soon.

en_definition: basic medical help given to someone who is suddenly hurt or sick
example1: The teacher gave first aid to the injured student.
example1_ru: Учитель оказал первую помощь травмированному студенту.
example2: Everyone on the trip learned basic first aid.

en_definition: a strip of cloth used to cover a wound
example1: She put a bandage on her cut finger.
example1_ru: Она наложила бинт на порезанный палец.
example2: The nurse changed the bandage every morning.

en_definition: to become healthy again after being ill
example1: I hope you get better soon after the flu.
example1_ru: Надеюсь, ты скоро поправишься после гриппа.
example2: It took him a week to get better after the surgery.

en_definition: slightly cold, often in an unpleasant way
example1: It's a bit chilly outside, so take a jacket.
example1_ru: На улице немного прохладно, так что возьми куртку.
example2: The evenings get chilly in early autumn.

en_definition: extremely hot, informally used for weather
example1: It was boiling outside, so we stayed indoors.
example1_ru: На улице было очень жарко, поэтому мы остались дома.
example2: The classroom felt boiling without air conditioning.

en_definition: covered with clouds, with no sunshine
example1: The sky was overcast all afternoon.
example1_ru: Небо было пасмурным весь день.
example2: It stayed overcast, but it never actually rained.
